output_debit_classifier.reason: Ford discounted a non-interest-bearing note at the bank. The transaction text states management has determined this is a collateralized borrowing, meaning the note receivable is not derecognized (risks and rewards not transferred). Instead, Ford treats the proceeds as a loan secured by the note. The bank advances cash equal to the face value less discount charges for the remaining 40 days (90 - 50). Using actual/365 day-count: discount = 100,000 × 0.15 × (40/365) = 1,643.84. Net proceeds = 100,000 - 1,643.84 = 98,356.16. Ford debits Cash for the net proceeds, debits Interest Expense for the discount charges, and credits Bank Loan Payable for the face value (the amount Ford will owe the bank when the note matures). The original Notes Receivable account is unaffected because the note remains Ford's asset under the collateralized borrowing treatment.

output_entry_builder.reason: Ford discounted a 90-day, $100,000 non-interest-bearing note at the bank 50 days after issue. With 40 days remaining, the bank applied a 15% annual discount rate. Management determined this is a collateralized borrowing, so the note receivable stays on Ford's books and a liability is created. Discount charges = 100,000 × 0.15 × (40/365) = 1,643.84. Net proceeds = 100,000 - 1,643.84 = 98,356.16. Ford receives cash (net proceeds), recognizes interest expense (discount charges), and records a liability to the bank (face value of note).
